behavior_in_task_description.reason: All test-asserted specifics are discoverable from instruction.md: file paths (src/libs/pdfjs/index.tsx, worker.ts, pdf.worker.ts, the two viewer components, CLAUDE.md), the `workerSrc` template literal format with npmmirror URL, `ensureWorker()` function name, `GlobalWorkerOptions.workerSrc`, imports from `@/libs/pdfjs`, 'Trigger condition', 'Workflow:', 'ToolSearch' — all explicitly mentioned in the instructions.

no_solution_leakage.reason: The instruction explicitly tells the agent exactly what to implement: 'Define a const workerSrc template literal using the format https://registry.npmmirror.com/pdfjs-dist/${pdfjs.version}/files/build/pdf.worker.min.mjs', 'Define an ensureWorker() function', 'Export a wrapper Document component that calls ensureWorker() in its body', and specifies exact CLAUDE.md restructuring including 'Step 1 must mention using ToolSearch'. This is a detailed implementation specification, not just a descriptio

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests assert on gold-specific implementation details: `const workerSrc` as a template literal (regex match), the exact function name `ensureWorker()`, and that it appears as a call `ensureWorker()` in the file. An alternative correct fix could use a different variable name (e.g., `WORKER_URL`), a different function name (e.g., `initWorker`), or set workerSrc inline in the Document wrapper without a separate function — all would fix the bug but fail the tests.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: test_ensure_worker_sets_src extracts the workerSrc template and executes it via Node subprocess to verify it correctly sets workerSrc with version interpolation. While many tests do use text matching (imports, CLAUDE.md content), the core behavioral test actually executes code, so not every test is purely text-based.

test_not_tautological.reason: The f2p tests (test_ensure_worker_sets_src) cannot be satisfied by a stub — the Node execution verifies that the extracted template literal actually produces a URL containing the version and 'pdf.worker'. The old file deletion tests require actual file removal. The import tests require real content changes in existing files.

anti_cheating_measures.reason: The solution is present at /workspace/lobe-chat in the container (the gold patch is applied via solve.sh). The agent could potentially read /solution/solve.sh or the git diff to discover the exact patch. Additionally, the Dockerfile doesn't remove the solution directory.

behavior_in_tests.reason: All behaviors from instruction.md are tested: unified module creation with workerSrc/ensureWorker/Document wrapper, old file deletion, import updates in both viewer components, CLAUDE.md restructuring with Trigger condition/Workflow/ToolSearch, plus pass-to-pass CI checks (lint, tests, circular deps).

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are well-named (test_ensure_worker_sets_src, test_old_worker_files_removed, etc.), have docstrings explaining rationale, and are grouped with a clear separator comment for pass-to-pass CI/CD checks.

structured_data_schema.reason: Task produces source code files and documentation, not structured data like JSON/CSV/API/DB schemas.

no_typos.reason: All file paths, variable names, URLs, and commands in instruction.md match what's expected in the tests without typos.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: The instruction specifies exact implementation details: the exact URL format for workerSrc, exact function name 'ensureWorker()', exact variable name 'workerSrc', exact structure of CLAUDE.md changes including 'Step 1 must mention using ToolSearch'. This goes beyond identifying what's broken and prescribes the exact solution.

config_edit_scope_bounded.reason: This is primarily a code task, not an agentmd-edits task focused on config files.
